data.content:
**Diagnosis:** The `GenerateSampleCode` target writes `GeneratedInfo.cs` to disk but never adds it to the `Compile` item group, so Roslyn doesn't know about it.

**Fix:** Add the generated file to `Compile` and register it in `FileWrites` for clean support.

arguments.new_str:
<Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />

    <!-- Include the generated file in compilation and register for Clean -->
    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
      <FileWrites Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
    </ItemGroup>
  </Target>

data.reasoningText:
The issue is clear. The `GenerateSampleCode` target runs `BeforeTargets="CoreCompile;BeforeCompile"` and generates the file, but never adds it to the `Compile` item group. The file is written to disk but Roslyn doesn't know about it.

The fix is to add the generated file to the `Compile` item group inside the target, and also register it in `FileWrites` for clean support.

This is the classic "including-generated-files" pattern.

arguments.new_str:
<Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />

    <!-- Include the generated file in compilation and register for Clean -->
    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
      <FileWrites Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
    </ItemGroup>
  </Target>

  </Target>", "new_str": "    <Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />

    <ItemGroup>

    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
      <FileWrites Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
    </ItemGroup>
  </Target>

data.content:
**Root cause:** The `GenerateSampleCode` target wrote the `.cs` file to disk but never added it to the `Compile` item group. MSBuild globs expand at **evaluation time** (before any targets run), so the file generated at **execution time** was invisible to the compiler.

**Fix applied** (lines added to the target in `TestProject.csproj`):
- `<Compile Include="..."/>` — tells Roslyn to compile the generated file
- `<FileWrites Include="..."/>` — registers it so `dotnet clean` removes it
